lyrics:
I traced the lines of maybe on the walls I used to know
Built a house of hesitation from the things I wouldn’t show
I told myself it’s safer not to reach beyond the past
Where “Never Only After” seemed much better than the crash

“Never Only After” becomes a shuttered door
A place where you can hide
And lock yourself inside
“Ever Only After” is an offer to explore
The courage to try-
Where **your** dreams reside…
“Ever Only After”

If the setting of the sun should lose its rosy hue
The nighthawk’s call forgets the sadness it alone once knew
The twinkling stars refuse to move in transit ’cross the sky
Then “Ever Only After” will I stop and wonder why

As days dissolve and seasons fly like moments in the rain
The cadence of a distant song still longing for refrain
My eyes of love look past the years in morning’s sweet review
Then “Ever Only After” will I share myself with you

In the chaos of “or,” we lost what we were for…
Until “Ever Only After”
Unlocked the shuttered door…
“Ever Only After”
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track presents a sparse, intimate texture centered around a clean acoustic guitar and a gentle, earnest male vocal, creating a contemplative and slightly melancholic atmosphere. The energy remains low and steady, maintaining a narrative, ballad-like feel that builds subtly in the choruses through the addition of soft, layered background harmonies. The production is spacious and polished, with each element sitting clearly in the mix; the lead vocal is present and warm, while supporting instruments provide depth without creating density. A defining quality is the shift into a wordless, layered vocal harmony section near the end, which elevates the track's emotional weight and provides a moment of pure, ethereal reflection before resolving quietly.

review.review_text: The lyrical conceit here is genuinely clever: 'Never Only After' as a shuttered door versus 'Ever Only After' as an invitation, and PCBender earns that wordplay by letting it breathe across a patient 4/4 structure in C major rather than rushing to the payoff. The acoustic guitar stays clean and uncluttered throughout, giving the vocal room to carry the weight, and it does, especially in the chorus where the pledge 'Ever Only After will I share myself with you' lands with quiet conviction. What lifts the track above a straightforward confessional ballad is the wordless layered harmony section near the close, which functions the way a gospel choir does in a Bill Withers record: it says what words can't. At 136 BPM it moves with more purpose than the sparse production suggests, and that slight tension between the song's reflective tone and its underlying momentum keeps it from settling too comfortably.
